DEPT. 501: PLAINTIFF GREG BURGHART'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS RELATING TO: (1) DEFENDANTS' SPOLIATION OF EVIDENCE; AND (2) DEFENDANTS' FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THIS COURT'S ORDER TO PRESERVE EVIDENCE; AND REQUEST FOR EVIDENTIARY AND MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. COURT FINDS DEFENDANTS COMPLIED WITH THE COURT'S ORDER IN GOOD FAITH. FURTHERMORE, NO INTENTIONAL DESTRUCTION OF EVIDENCE OCCURRED. ORAL REQUEST FOR JURY INSTRUCTION IS DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: JUDITH A. DEALBA, CSR NO. 5709, BUS. TEL: 650-571-1473. (D501)

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, GRANTED. THE NUMBER OF DISCOVERY MOTIONS AND EX-PARTE APPLICATIONS IN THIS CASE, WHERE ISSUE OF LACK OF PERMITS FOR THE DWELLING PREMISES DOES NOT SEEM TO BE IN DISPUTE, CONSTITUTES EXTRAORDINARY CIRCUMSTANCES WARRANTING APPOINTMENT OF REFEREE. ADDITIONALLY, TRANSCRIPTS OF THE DEPOSITIONS AND PARTIES ARGUMENTS IN REGARDS TO THE DEPOSITIONS INDICATE ADDITIONAL DISCOVERY DISPUTES WILL BE PRESENTED TO THE COURT. PARTIES SHOULD PROVIDE THE COURT WITH 3 NOMINATIONS FOR REFEREE BY 4/21/14 5:00 PM. PROPOSSED ORDER SUBMITTED TO THE COURT. JUDGE LILLIAN SING, CLERK: BERTHA PAREDES; REPORTER JUDITH DEALBA CSR. 5709 (501)

Off Calendar Jun-27-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Judith A. DeAlba, CSR No. 5709, Bus. Tel: 650-571-1473. (D501)

Appearances:
Ian A. Hansen, Esq. of Hooshmand Law Group (415-318-5709) is present for the plaintiff/moving party
Corie A. Edwards, Esq. of Vogl Meredith Burke LLP (415-398-0200) is present for defendant/opposing party

Court hears arguments from both sides. Court rules now as follows:

PLAINTIFF GREG BURGHART's Motion For Sanctions Relating To: (1) Defendants' Spoliation Of Evidence; And (2) Defendants' Failure To Comply With This Court'S Order To Preserve Evidence; and Request For Evidentiary And Monetary Sanctions are DENIED. Court finds Defendants complied with the Court's order in good faith. Furthermore, no intentional destruction of evidence occurred. Oral request for jury instruction is DENIED. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Second Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For The Appointment Of A Discovery Referee For The Supervision Of The Conduct Of Written Discovery, Depositions, And Related Activities
Off Calendar Apr-17-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Hooshmand, Esq. Attorney at Law (415-318-5709) for Greg Burghart Plaintiff and opposing party.
Corie A. Edwards, Esq. of Vogl, Meredith, Burke LLP (415-398-0200)for Daniel O'neil and Cindy O'neil Defendants moving party.

DEFENDANT DANIEL O'NEILL and CINDY O'NEILL's Second Amended Motion For The Appointment Of A Discovery Referee For The Supervision Of The Conduct Of Written Discovery, Depositions, And Related Activities

Argued. The court adopts the tentative rulings as follows: GRANTED. The number of discovery motions and ex-parte applications in this case, where issue of lack of permits for the dwelling premises does not seem to be in dispute, constitutes extraordinary circumstances warranting appointment of referee. Additionally, transcripts of the depositions and parties arguments in regards to the depositions indicate additional discovery disputes will be presented to the Court. Parties should provide the Court with 3 nominations for referee by 4/21/14 5:00 pm. Propossed order submitted to the court. Judge Lillian Sing, Clerk: Bertha Paredes; reporter Judith DeAlba CSR. 5709 (501)
